mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution that provides a robust platform for deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters in enterprise environments. It leverages the Tanzu Framework and Carvel packaging to enhance the user experience and integration. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with VMware's ecosystem.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is essential for managing Tanzu Kubernetes Grid.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xvf tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv tanzu /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Tanzu CLI tarball.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the Tanzu CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary plugins.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> Initialize the Tanzu CLI and install required plugins.

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Tanzu Kubernetes cluster with specified parameters.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-1

```

> Create a cluster named 'my-cluster' with 3 nodes.
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-1

steps.title: Configure Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up the kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> Fetch the kubeconfig for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of the nodes in the cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

kubectl delete namespace my-cluster-namespace

```

> Delete the cluster named 'my-cluster'.
> Remove any associated namespaces.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, drain the nodes, perform the upgrade, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

tanzu cluster backup my-cluster

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup the cluster before upgrading.
> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade the cluster to the new Kubernetes version.
> Verify that the nodes are upgraded successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: timeout waiting for cluster to be ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the cluster has sufficient resources (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl and ensure it is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this action'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not be configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check and update RBAC settings for the user or service account.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.29, improved CLI performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to backup your clusters and review the new RBAC policies.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing the nodes in 'Ready' status.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before installation.
